summary:
## Metadata
- File path: .github/workflows/diffdoc-summarize.yml
- File name: diffdoc-summarize.yml
- Extension: .yml
- Inferred language/type: GitHub Actions workflow YAML
- Content hash: 5b2db1d49de70cd72ab0ba4533119124
- Line count: 32
- Byte size: 936
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: DiffDoc Summaries, summarize, actions/checkout@v4, sullyTheDev/diffdoc-action@main
- External dependencies: GitHub Actions runtime, actions/checkout@v4, sullyTheDev/diffdoc-action@main, GitHub secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY, GitHub vars.AI_PROVIDER, GitHub vars.DIFFDOC_BASE_DIR, GitHub vars.CLOUD_LLM_ENDPOINT, GitHub vars.CLOUD_CHAT_MODEL
- Internal dependencies: .diffdoc/manifest.json, .diffdoc/summaries/**, .diffdoc/vectra/**
- Public API/exports: None identified.

## Purpose
This workflow updates DiffDoc-generated summaries and the DiffDoc manifest when changes are pushed to the main branch. It automates summary generation through a DiffDoc action and commits or updates repository-managed DiffDoc artifacts.

## User-Visible Behavior
On pushes to `main`, the workflow runs automatically unless the only changes are within ignored DiffDoc output paths. It checks out the full repository history, then runs the DiffDoc action to update summary artifacts. The workflow uses repository secrets and variables to configure the AI provider, model, endpoint, and base directory.

## Business Rules
- Triggers only on `push` events to the `main` branch.
- Ignores pushes that modify only:
  - `.diffdoc/manifest.json`
  - `.diffdoc/summaries/**`
  - `.diffdoc/vectra/**`
- Grants `contents: write` permission, implying the workflow is allowed to update repository contents.
- Uses a single concurrency group named `diffdoc-summarize-main`.
- Does not cancel in-progress runs when a new run starts for the same group.
- Uses `fetch-depth: 0` to ensure the checkout includes full history.
- Defaults `ai-provider` to `cloud` when `vars.AI_PROVIDER` is unset.
- Defaults `base-dir` to `.diffdoc` when `vars.DIFFDOC_BASE_DIR` is unset.
- Defaults `cloud-llm-endpoint` to `https://api.openai.com/v1` when `vars.CLOUD_LLM_ENDPOINT` is unset.
- Defaults `cloud-chat-model` to `gpt-4o-mini` when `vars.CLOUD_CHAT_MODEL` is unset.
- Sets `summarize-concurrency` to `2`.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs:
- Git push events on `main`
- Repository contents at checkout time
- GitHub secret `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- GitHub variables for AI provider, base directory, LLM endpoint, and chat model
- Existing DiffDoc files under `.diffdoc`

Outputs:
- Updated DiffDoc manifest and summary artifacts produced by the DiffDoc action
- Repository content changes written back by the workflow/action
- Workflow run status and logs

## Side Effects
- Checks out the repository in the GitHub Actions runner.
- Invokes an external GitHub Action that may generate or update files.
- Writes repository contents, enabled by `contents: write`.
- Produces workflow logs and action output.
- May update `.diffdoc` artifacts in the repository working tree.

## Error And Edge Cases
- If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is missing or invalid, the DiffDoc action may fail.
- If the configured AI provider, endpoint, or model is invalid, summary generation may fail.
- If the repository has no relevant changes outside ignored paths, the workflow will not trigger.
- If checkout or the external action fails, the job fails.
- If variables are unset, the workflow falls back to its configured defaults.
- Concurrency prevents overlapping runs in the same group, but in-progress runs are not canceled.

## Dependencies
- GitHub Actions workflow engine
- `actions/checkout@v4`
- `sullyTheDev/diffdoc-action@main`
- GitHub secrets and variables:
  - `secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY`
  - `vars.AI_PROVIDER`
  - `vars.DIFFDOC_BASE_DIR`
  - `vars.CLOUD_LLM_ENDPOINT`
  - `vars.CLOUD_CHAT_MODEL`
- Repository paths under `.diffdoc`

## Operational Notes
- The workflow is intended for automated maintenance on `main`, not manual execution.
- Full history checkout may increase runtime and bandwidth compared with shallow clones.
- The external DiffDoc action is pinned to a moving `main` branch reference, which can change behavior over time.
- Because `contents: write` is enabled, the workflow should be reviewed carefully for repository mutation safety.
- The `summarize-concurrency` setting of `2` suggests the action may process multiple summaries in parallel.
